summary: Reconciled the remaining cross-pane protocol wording so historical proposed L69/L70 doctrine references explicitly point to landed L81/L82, while preserving canonical L69/L70 ownership.

evidence.command: rg -n "does not define L69 doctrine wording|does not define L70 doctrine wording|L69 doctrine wording" .flywheel/PLANS/cross-pane-protocol-2026-05-01 .flywheel/rules AGENTS.md README.md docs site .beads/issues.jsonl || true
evidence.result: no stale unreconciled doctrine wording remains

evidence.command: rg -n "proposed L69|proposed L70|landed as L81|landed as L82|available IDs for these two doctrines" .flywheel/PLANS/cross-pane-protocol-2026-05-01 .flywheel/rules AGENTS.md README.md docs site .beads/issues.jsonl
evidence.result: remaining hits are historical/reconciliation contexts, including explicit L81/L82 landing and L69/L70 unavailable wording
empty output; no duplicate L-rule IDs

evidence.command: jq -r 'select(.status != "closed") | select((.description // "" | test("proposed L69|proposed L70|does not define L69|does not define L70|L69 doctrine|L70 doctrine")) or (.title // "" | test("proposed L69|proposed L70|does not define L69|does not define L70|L69 doctrine|L70 doctrine"))) | [.id,.status,.title] | @tsv' .beads/issues.jsonl
evidence.result: only flywheel-lhi4 remains; its description is the reconciliation bead itself

notes: The historical filename 01-L69-DOCTRINE-AND-STATE-MACHINE.md is intentionally preserved as source provenance; its body says the doctrine landed as L81 and CLI scoping landed as L82.
Canonical L69 remains ORCH-PROBE-AGENT-CONTEXT and canonical L70 remains ORCH-NO-PUNT.
